title: frangula nelsonii
intro: Frangula nelsonii, also known as Nelson's buckthorn, is a species of deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include acting as a laxative and treating digestive issues, such as constipation. The plant's health benefits include stimulating bowel movements, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Frangula nelsonii can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat gastrointestinal issues and maintain overall digestive health.
